input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to specify values for `Light` and `Dark`:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">
        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="24"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#F0F0F0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device's current `AppTheme` and re-evaluates bindings automatically when the user toggles system dark mode.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:

```csharp
// Extension method on any BindableObject
label.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);
page.SetAppThemeColor(VisualElement.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#FFFFFF"), Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

// For non-color properties (e.g., an image source):
image.SetAppTheme(Image.SourceProperty, "light_logo.png", "dark_logo.png");
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concept | Detail |
|---------|--------|
| Trigger | Responds to `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark`) |
| Live update | Yes — switches at runtime when the OS theme changes |
| Works with | Any bindable property (colors, images, strings, etc.) |
| Resources approach | You can also define `AppThemeBinding` inside `ResourceDictionary` styles for reuse |

No additional setup is needed — `AppThemeBinding` is built into MAUI's markup extensions.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about .NET MAUI's `AppThemeBinding` markup extension for automatic light/dark theme switching. This is a knowledge/explanation question, not a code task on a repository. I'll provide a concise, practical example.
